clinical_history: This ECG from an adult man is most consistent with which ONE of the following diagnoses?
diagnosis: This ECG shows sinus rhythm with a prolonged PR interval of 230 ms, indicative of first-degree AV delay ("block"). The following constellation of findings is highly specific for severe left ventricular hypertrophy (LVH): 1) very prominent voltage, including R=20 mm in aVL; 2) marked left atrial abnormality (see V1);  an intraventricular delay (IVCD)  with a QRS duration of about 130 ms (which may progress to a frank left bundle branch block); 4) characteristic ST-T changes in I and aVL (formerly called an LV "strain" pattern"); 5) left axis deviation. The high take-off of the J point/ST-T in leads V1-V3, mimicking ischemia, is often seen in this context, possibly due to relatively early repolarization of the right ventricle. A delayed precordial transition zone is also present. The patient had critical aortic stenosis. See Wave-Maven cases for discussion of other choices.
